commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=449cc7e41b399ee65c34c613c721d02d15375266)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `runReplyAgent` response-usage footer fallback handling and adds a Telegram-specific layout to the default usage-bar template.

PR surface: Source +31. Total +31 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the patch defect: source inspection shows a valid usage template that renders empty for total-only usage still falls through to the same formatter that rejects total-only data. No live Telegram/Docker reproduction of the original reporter environment was established in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This external PR needs author or maintainer-supplied real Telegram proof and maintainer comparison with overlapping same-issue candidates before any merge path.

target.title: Add snapshot provider proof plugin
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a bundled `snapshot` plugin scaffold under `extensions/snapshot` without exposing a public CLI or public provider barrel yet.
- Adds a private local `SqliteSnapshotProvider` proof backed by a manifest + compact `database.sqlite` artifact repository.
- Shares the SQLite `VACUUM INTO` primitive with the existing backup path through `@openclaw/memory-host-sdk`, including sqlite-vec loading and the same compact-snapshot behavior.
- Keeps the lockfile scoped to workspace links only; the draft plugin no longer pulls a published `openclaw` package or changes existing `undici` resolution.
- Proves committed WAL-mode state is captured without copying live `-wal`/`-shm` sidecars.
- Handles failed creates, same-millisecond snapshot id collisions, signed SQLite `user_version`, and stale restore-target WAL sidecars.

## Scope

This is PR 1 from the snapshot RFC roadmap: provider proof only. It intentionally does not add `openclaw snapshot ...` commands, backup command integration, remote storage, leases, scheduling, or failover orchestration.

RFC context: https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/20
Related storage discussion: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/90370

## Verification

- `pnpm install --ignore-scripts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/snapshot/src/snapshot-provider.test.ts` - passed, 1 file / 6 tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/sqlite-vec.test.ts` - passed, 1 file / 9 tests
- `node_modules\.bin\oxlint packages\memory-host-sdk\src\host\sqlite-snapshot.ts packages\memory-host-sdk\src\engine-storage.ts extensions\snapshot\src\local-repository.ts src\infra\backup-create.ts` - passed
- `node --import tsx -e "await import('./extensions/snapshot/src/local-repository.ts'); await import('./packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/sqlite-snapshot.ts'); await import('./src/infra/backup-create.ts'); console.log('imports ok')"` - passed
- `git diff --check` - passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/backup-create.test.ts` - 37/38 passed; the remaining Windows `EBUSY` cleanup failure reproduces unchanged on a clean `origin/main` worktree at `C:\tmp\openclaw-main-backup-baseline`, so it is treated as base noise rather than this patch.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p extensions/snapshot/tsconfig.json --noEmit` - blocked by unrelated current worktree/package-resolution errors in `packages/gateway-client`, `@openclaw/net-policy`, and `dist/plugin-sdk` duplicate `EventStream` declarations before any snapshot-specific error.
- `bash /c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --output .artifacts/snapshot-pr1-review-fix-autoreview.txt` - timed out after 5 minutes; partial output did not produce a completed actionable finding list and captured the same base `EBUSY` backup cleanup failure.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: SQLite snapshot provider can create, verify, list, and internally restore compact snapshot artifacts from SQLite databases, including WAL-mode state, while sharing the compact SQLite snapshot primitive used by backup.
Real environment tested: Windows source checkout, Node/Vitest extension test runner against `node:sqlite`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/snapshot/src/snapshot-provider.test.ts`
Evidence after fix: Test suite creates a WAL-mode source database, commits a row after `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`, snapshots via the local provider, verifies the manifest and artifact, restores to a fresh path, and reads both pre-WAL and committed-in-WAL rows from the restored database. It also covers failed create cleanup, artifact tamper detection, signed `user_version`, same-millisecond snapshot IDs, and stale restore-target WAL sidecar removal.
Observed result after fix: 1 test file passed; 6 provider tests passed.
What was not tested: Public `openclaw snapshot` CLI, backup command integration, object storage providers, scheduled snapshots, leases, and fresh OpenClaw state-dir boot proof; those are intentionally later PRs.
target.author: giodl73-repo
